L1.area: External tool and server configuration processing logic

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where user-provided server configuration input is processed using a dynamic code evaluation mechanism rather than a safe data parser. An attacker can craft a malicious configuration string that, when submitted via the API, causes arbitrary JavaScript to execute with full Node.js runtime privileges, enabling system command execution, file system access, and data exfiltration.

L3.area: External tool and server configuration processing logic
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where user-provided server configuration input is processed using a dynamic code evaluation mechanism rather than a safe data parser. An attacker can craft a malicious configuration string that, when submitted via the API, causes arbitrary JavaScript to execute with full Node.js runtime privileges, enabling system command execution, file system access, and data exfiltration.

ground_truth.reason: The convertToValidJSONString function uses the Function() constructor to evaluate user-provided MCP server configuration strings, allowing arbitrary JavaScript code execution with full Node.js runtime privileges.
